---
title: "Flagship Visualization: Wildfire Disruption and Chronic Absenteeism"
format:
  html:
    toc: false
    code-fold: true
    fig-width: 13
    fig-height: 8
    fig-dpi: 320
  pdf:
    fig-width: 13
    fig-height: 8
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
editor: visual
---

```{r setup}
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(sf)
library(scales)
library(grid)

theme_set(theme_minimal(base_family = "sans", base_size = 12))

analysis_years <- c(2018, 2019)
analysis_year_labels <- c("2017-18", "2018-19")

to_number <- function(x) {
  readr::parse_number(as.character(x), na = c("", "NA", "N/A", "NULL", "*"))
}

safe_divide <- function(num, den) {
  if_else(!is.na(den) & den > 0, num / den, NA_real_)
}

clean_cds_code <- function(x) {
  digits <- str_remove_all(coalesce(as.character(x), ""), "\\D")
  if_else(digits == "", NA_character_, str_pad(digits, width = 14, side = "left", pad = "0"))
}

pad_code <- function(x, width) {
  digits <- str_remove_all(coalesce(as.character(x), ""), "\\D")
  if_else(digits == "", NA_character_, str_pad(digits, width = width, side = "left", pad = "0"))
}

first_existing_path <- function(candidates, missing_message) {
  path <- candidates[file.exists(candidates)][1]
  if (is.na(path)) {
    stop(missing_message)
  }
  path
}

label_short_count <- function(x) {
  case_when(
    is.na(x) ~ NA_character_,
    abs(x) >= 999500 ~ str_c(number(x / 1000000, accuracy = 0.1, trim = TRUE), "M"),
    x >= 1000 ~ str_c(number(x / 1000, accuracy = 1, trim = TRUE), "K"),
    TRUE ~ number(x, accuracy = 1, trim = TRUE)
  )
}
```

```{r load-and-summarize-closures}
closure_path <- first_existing_path(
  c(
    file.path("..", "data", "raw", "school_fire", "annual_school_closures_standardized_test_scores_ca_2003_2019.csv"),
    file.path("data", "raw", "school_fire", "annual_school_closures_standardized_test_scores_ca_2003_2019.csv")
  ),
  "Could not find the school closure CSV under data/raw/school_fire/."
)

closures <- readr::read_csv(
  closure_path,
  col_types = readr::cols(
    cds_code = readr::col_character(),
    .default = readr::col_guess()
  ),
  show_col_types = FALSE
) |>
  clean_names()

if ("days_wildfires" %in% names(closures) && !"days_wildfire" %in% names(closures)) {
  closures <- closures |>
    rename(days_wildfire = days_wildfires)
}

primary_cause_vars <- c(
  "days_wildfire",
  "days_natural_disasters_weather",
  "days_infrastructure",
  "days_student_safety",
  "days_other"
)

closure_numeric_vars <- c(
  primary_cause_vars,
  "days_quarantine",
  "days_safety_other",
  "days_total",
  "total_enrollment"
)

closures_clean <- closures |>
  mutate(
    year = as.integer(year),
    closure_year = year,
    county = str_squish(str_to_title(county)),
    cds_code = clean_cds_code(cds_code),
    cd_code = str_sub(cds_code, 1, 7),
    across(any_of(closure_numeric_vars), to_number),
    across(any_of(primary_cause_vars), ~ replace_na(.x, 0)),
    days_total = replace_na(days_total, 0),
    total_enrollment = replace_na(total_enrollment, 0),
    wildfire_student_days = days_wildfire * total_enrollment
  )

county_wildfire <- closures_clean |>
  filter(year %in% analysis_years) |>
  group_by(county) |>
  summarise(
    schools = n_distinct(cds_code),
    districts = n_distinct(cd_code),
    enrolled_student_years = sum(total_enrollment, na.rm = TRUE),
    wildfire_days = sum(days_wildfire, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_closure_days = sum(days_total, na.rm = TRUE),
    wildfire_student_days = sum(wildfire_student_days, na.rm = TRUE),
    wildfire_days_per_1k_students = 1000 * safe_divide(
      wildfire_student_days,
      enrolled_student_years
    ),
    wildfire_share_of_closure_days = safe_divide(wildfire_days, total_closure_days),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(county), county != "")
```

```{r load-and-summarize-chronic-absenteeism}
resolve_attendance_source <- function(file_name, url) {
  candidates <- c(
    file.path("..", "data", "raw", "cde_attendance", file_name),
    file.path("data", "raw", "cde_attendance", file_name)
  )

  local_source <- candidates[file.exists(candidates)][1]
  if (!is.na(local_source)) {
    return(local_source)
  }

  url
}

standardize_chronic_absenteeism_names <- function(data) {
  if ("charter_yn" %in% names(data) && !"charter" %in% names(data)) {
    data <- data |>
      rename(charter = charter_yn)
  }

  if (!"dass" %in% names(data)) {
    data <- data |>
      mutate(dass = "All")
  }

  eligible_col <- names(data)[str_detect(names(data), "^chronic_absenteeism_eligible")]
  if (
    !"chronic_absenteeism_eligible_cumulative_enrollment" %in% names(data) &&
      length(eligible_col) > 0
  ) {
    data <- data |>
      rename(chronic_absenteeism_eligible_cumulative_enrollment = all_of(eligible_col[1]))
  }

  if ("chronic_absenteeism_rate" %in% names(data)) {
    data <- data |>
      rename(chronic_absenteeism_rate_reported = chronic_absenteeism_rate)
  }

  data
}

read_tsv_source <- function(source) {
  if (file.exists(source)) {
    return(
      readr::read_tsv(
        source,
        col_types = readr::cols(.default = readr::col_character()),
        na = c("", "NA"),
        show_col_types = FALSE
      )
    )
  }

  old_timeout <- getOption("timeout")
  options(timeout = 45)
  on.exit(options(timeout = old_timeout), add = TRUE)

  lines <- readLines(source, warn = FALSE)
  lines <- iconv(lines, from = "", to = "UTF-8", sub = "byte")

  if (
    length(lines) < 2 ||
      str_detect(str_to_lower(lines[1]), "<html|<!doctype|wafalert")
  ) {
    stop("Attendance source did not return a tab-delimited data file.")
  }

  readr::read_tsv(
    I(paste(lines, collapse = "\n")),
    col_types = readr::cols(.default = readr::col_character()),
    na = c("", "NA"),
    show_col_types = FALSE
  )
}

read_chronic_absenteeism_file <- function(file_name, url, closure_year) {
  source <- resolve_attendance_source(file_name, url)

  read_tsv_source(source) |>
    clean_names() |>
    standardize_chronic_absenteeism_names() |>
    mutate(closure_year = closure_year)
}

chronic_sources <- tibble(
  closure_year = analysis_years,
  file_name = c(
    "chronicabsenteeism18.txt",
    "chronicabsenteeism19.txt"
  ),
  url = c(
    "https://www3.cde.ca.gov/demo-downloads/attendance/chronicabsenteeism18.txt",
    "https://www3.cde.ca.gov/demo-downloads/attendance/chronicabsenteeism19.txt"
  )
)

chronic_numeric_vars <- c(
  "chronic_absenteeism_eligible_cumulative_enrollment",
  "chronic_absenteeism_count",
  "chronic_absenteeism_rate_reported"
)

county_chronic <- chronic_sources |>
  mutate(data = pmap(list(file_name, url, closure_year), read_chronic_absenteeism_file)) |>
  select(data) |>
  unnest(cols = data) |>
  filter(
    aggregate_level == "C",
    reporting_category == "TA",
    str_to_lower(charter) == "all",
    str_to_lower(dass) == "all"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    county_name = str_squish(str_to_title(county_name)),
    across(all_of(chronic_numeric_vars), to_number)
  ) |>
  group_by(county_name) |>
  summarise(
    chronic_absenteeism_eligible_enrollment = sum(
      chronic_absenteeism_eligible_cumulative_enrollment,
      na.rm = TRUE
    ),
    chronic_absenteeism_count = sum(chronic_absenteeism_count, na.rm = TRUE),
    pooled_chronic_absenteeism_rate = safe_divide(
      chronic_absenteeism_count,
      chronic_absenteeism_eligible_enrollment
    ),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

```{r prepare-map-data}
load_ca_counties <- function() {
  if (!requireNamespace("sf", quietly = TRUE)) {
    stop("Package 'sf' is required for the county map.")
  }

  old_timeout <- getOption("timeout")
  options(timeout = 60)
  on.exit(options(timeout = old_timeout), add = TRUE)

  county_zip_url <- "https://www2.census.gov/geo/tiger/GENZ2018/shp/cb_2018_us_county_500k.zip"
  zip_path <- tempfile(fileext = ".zip")
  unzip_dir <- tempfile()
  dir.create(unzip_dir)

  download.file(county_zip_url, zip_path, mode = "wb", quiet = TRUE)
  unzip(zip_path, exdir = unzip_dir)
  shp_path <- list.files(unzip_dir, pattern = "\\.shp$", full.names = TRUE)[1]

  sf::st_read(shp_path, quiet = TRUE) |>
    filter(STATEFP == "06") |>
    sf::st_transform(3310)
}

ca_counties <- load_ca_counties()

county_map <- ca_counties |>
  left_join(county_wildfire, by = c("NAME" = "county")) |>
  left_join(county_chronic, by = c("NAME" = "county_name")) |>
  mutate(
    wildfire_student_days = replace_na(wildfire_student_days, 0),
    wildfire_days = replace_na(wildfire_days, 0),
    enrolled_student_years = replace_na(enrolled_student_years, 0),
    wildfire_days_per_1k_students = safe_divide(
      1000 * wildfire_student_days,
      enrolled_student_years
    )
  )

county_fire_cutoff <- county_map |>
  st_drop_geometry() |>
  filter(wildfire_student_days > 0) |>
  summarise(cutoff = quantile(wildfire_student_days, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)) |>
  pull(cutoff)

county_absenteeism_median <- county_map |>
  st_drop_geometry() |>
  summarise(median_rate = median(pooled_chronic_absenteeism_rate, na.rm = TRUE)) |>
  pull(median_rate)

county_map <- county_map |>
  mutate(
    top_wildfire_burden = wildfire_student_days >= county_fire_cutoff,
    overlap_group = case_when(
      top_wildfire_burden & pooled_chronic_absenteeism_rate >= county_absenteeism_median ~
        "High wildfire, high absenteeism",
      top_wildfire_burden & pooled_chronic_absenteeism_rate < county_absenteeism_median ~
        "High wildfire, lower absenteeism",
      !top_wildfire_burden & pooled_chronic_absenteeism_rate >= county_absenteeism_median ~
        "Lower wildfire, high absenteeism",
      TRUE ~ "Lower wildfire, lower absenteeism"
    )
  )

county_points <- county_map |>
  sf::st_point_on_surface()

point_coords <- sf::st_coordinates(county_points)

county_points <- county_points |>
  mutate(
    point_x = point_coords[, 1],
    point_y = point_coords[, 2]
  )

county_plot_data <- county_points |>
  sf::st_drop_geometry()

label_positions <- tribble(
  ~NAME, ~label_x, ~label_y, ~label_note,
  "Ventura", -125000, -360000, "Ventura",
  "Sonoma", -355000, 85000, "Sonoma",
  "Los Angeles", 35000, -455000, "Los Angeles",
  "Butte", -230000, 220000, "Butte",
  "Lake", -347000, 145000, "Lake",
  "Napa", -304000, 27000, "Napa",
  "Inyo", 330000, -98000, "Inyo",
  "Nevada", -154000, 153000, "Nevada",
  "Plumas", -25000, 266000, "Plumas",
  "San Francisco", -354000, -47000, "San Francisco"
)

label_data <- county_plot_data |>
  inner_join(label_positions, by = "NAME")
```

```{r build-plot-objects}
map_fill_colors <- c(
  "#f5f1e6",
  "#d5d8be",
  "#9ab6aa",
  "#4f8f91",
  "#1f555b"
)

fire_orange <- "#b64a2f"
ink <- "#1f1f1f"
soft_ink <- "#555555"
paper <- "#fbfaf7"
county_line <- "#ffffff"

map_title_text <- "Wildfire disruption was concentrated in a few California counties"
map_subtitle_text <- str_wrap(
  str_c(
    "County color shows pooled chronic absenteeism. Circle area shows wildfire missed student-days, ",
    "with orange rings marking the top quarter of wildfire-affected counties."
  ),
  width = 118
)
relationship_title_text <- "The counties hit hardest by wildfire were not always the most chronically absent"
relationship_subtitle_text <- str_wrap(
  str_c(
    "The vertical line marks the top quarter of wildfire-affected counties. ",
    "The horizontal line marks the statewide county median chronic absenteeism rate."
  ),
  width = 118
)
shared_caption_text <- str_wrap(
  str_c(
    "Sources: California school closure records; California Department of Education chronic absenteeism files. ",
    "Wildfire missed student-days = wildfire closure days x school-year enrollment. ",
    "Descriptive county-level comparison, not a causal estimate."
  ),
  width = 176
)

legend_breaks_fire <- c(100000, 250000, 500000, 1000000)

base_map <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(
    data = county_map,
    aes(fill = pooled_chronic_absenteeism_rate),
    color = county_line,
    linewidth = 0.23
  ) +
  geom_sf(
    data = county_points |> filter(wildfire_student_days > 0),
    aes(size = wildfire_student_days),
    shape = 21,
    fill = "#ffffff",
    color = fire_orange,
    alpha = 0.72,
    stroke = 0.7
  ) +
  geom_sf(
    data = county_points |> filter(top_wildfire_burden),
    aes(size = wildfire_student_days),
    shape = 21,
    fill = NA,
    color = fire_orange,
    stroke = 1.45,
    alpha = 0.95
  ) +
  geom_segment(
    data = label_data,
    aes(x = label_x, y = label_y, xend = point_x, yend = point_y),
    linewidth = 0.25,
    color = "#767676"
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = label_data,
    aes(
      x = label_x,
      y = label_y,
      label = label_note
    ),
    family = "sans",
    fontface = "bold",
    size = 3.1,
    lineheight = 0.95,
    color = ink
  ) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = map_fill_colors,
    labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1),
    name = "Chronic\nabsenteeism",
    guide = guide_colorbar(
      title.position = "top",
      title.hjust = 0,
      barheight = unit(65, "pt"),
      barwidth = unit(8, "pt")
    )
  ) +
  scale_size_area(
    max_size = 24,
    breaks = legend_breaks_fire,
    labels = label_short_count,
    name = "Wildfire missed\nstudent-days",
    guide = guide_legend(
      title.position = "top",
      title.hjust = 0,
      override.aes = list(
        shape = 21,
        fill = "#ffffff",
        color = fire_orange,
        alpha = 0.75,
        stroke = 0.7
      )
    )
  ) +
  coord_sf(
    crs = sf::st_crs(3310),
    xlim = c(-410000, 585000),
    ylim = c(-640000, 485000),
    expand = FALSE,
    clip = "off"
  ) +
  theme_void(base_family = "sans", base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = paper, color = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = paper, color = NA),
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = paper, color = NA),
    legend.box.background = element_rect(fill = paper, color = NA),
    plot.title = element_text(
      family = "sans",
      face = "bold",
      size = 21,
      color = ink,
      hjust = 0,
      margin = margin(b = 7)
    ),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(
      family = "sans",
      size = 12.5,
      color = "#3d3d3d",
      lineheight = 1.12,
      hjust = 0,
      margin = margin(b = 18)
    ),
    plot.caption = element_text(
      family = "sans",
      size = 8.2,
      color = "#6a6a6a",
      lineheight = 1.08,
      hjust = 0,
      margin = margin(t = 10)
    ),
    plot.title.position = "plot",
    plot.caption.position = "plot",
    plot.margin = margin(4, 24, 12, 18),
    legend.position = c(0.895, 0.405),
    legend.justification = c(0, 0.5),
    legend.box = "vertical",
    legend.spacing.y = unit(8, "pt"),
    legend.title = element_text(
      family = "sans",
      face = "bold",
      size = 9.6,
      color = ink,
      lineheight = 1.02
    ),
    legend.text = element_text(
      family = "sans",
      size = 8.6,
      color = soft_ink
    )
  )

scatter_labels <- c(
  "Ventura",
  "Sonoma",
  "Los Angeles",
  "Lake",
  "Butte",
  "Inyo",
  "Nevada",
  "Plumas"
)

inset_data <- county_plot_data |>
  mutate(
    label = if_else(NAME %in% scatter_labels, NAME, NA_character_),
    x_label = case_when(
      NAME == "Inyo" ~ 13000,
      NAME == "Nevada" ~ 35000,
      NAME == "Plumas" ~ 9000,
      NAME == "Lake" ~ 155000,
      NAME == "Butte" ~ 520000,
      NAME == "Ventura" ~ 1300000,
      NAME == "Sonoma" ~ 900000,
      NAME == "Los Angeles" ~ 780000,
      TRUE ~ wildfire_student_days
    ),
    y_label = case_when(
      NAME == "Inyo" ~ pooled_chronic_absenteeism_rate + 0.015,
      NAME == "Nevada" ~ pooled_chronic_absenteeism_rate + 0.024,
      NAME == "Plumas" ~ pooled_chronic_absenteeism_rate - 0.018,
      NAME == "Lake" ~ pooled_chronic_absenteeism_rate + 0.012,
      NAME == "Butte" ~ pooled_chronic_absenteeism_rate + 0.010,
      NAME == "Ventura" ~ pooled_chronic_absenteeism_rate - 0.012,
      NAME == "Sonoma" ~ pooled_chronic_absenteeism_rate + 0.014,
      NAME == "Los Angeles" ~ pooled_chronic_absenteeism_rate - 0.014,
      TRUE ~ pooled_chronic_absenteeism_rate
    )
  )

inset_plot <- ggplot(
  inset_data,
  aes(x = wildfire_student_days, y = pooled_chronic_absenteeism_rate)
) +
  annotate(
    "rect",
    xmin = county_fire_cutoff,
    xmax = Inf,
    ymin = county_absenteeism_median,
    ymax = Inf,
    fill = fire_orange,
    alpha = 0.08
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = county_fire_cutoff,
    color = "#9a9a9a",
    linewidth = 0.35,
    linetype = "dashed"
  ) +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = county_absenteeism_median,
    color = "#9a9a9a",
    linewidth = 0.35,
    linetype = "dashed"
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(
      fill = overlap_group,
      size = chronic_absenteeism_eligible_enrollment
    ),
    shape = 21,
    color = "#ffffff",
    stroke = 0.35,
    alpha = 0.9
  ) +
  geom_segment(
    data = inset_data |> filter(!is.na(label)),
    aes(x = x_label, y = y_label, xend = wildfire_student_days, yend = pooled_chronic_absenteeism_rate),
    linewidth = 0.2,
    color = "#777777"
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = inset_data |> filter(!is.na(label)),
    aes(x = x_label, y = y_label, label = label),
    family = "sans",
    fontface = "bold",
    size = 2.35,
    color = ink,
    lineheight = 0.95
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = county_fire_cutoff * 1.28,
    y = 0.45,
    hjust = 0,
    family = "sans",
    fontface = "bold",
    size = 2.55,
    color = fire_orange,
    label = "High fire +\nhigher absenteeism"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    trans = pseudo_log_trans(base = 10),
    breaks = c(0, 10000, 100000, 1000000),
    labels = label_short_count,
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.03, 0.16))
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0.04, 0.515),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.03))
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "High wildfire, high absenteeism" = "#8f3b25",
      "High wildfire, lower absenteeism" = "#d17a45",
      "Lower wildfire, high absenteeism" = "#326f77",
      "Lower wildfire, lower absenteeism" = "#b8b8b8"
    ),
    guide = "none"
  ) +
  scale_size_area(max_size = 5.5, guide = "none") +
  labs(
    title = "The overlap is partial",
    x = "Wildfire missed student-days",
    y = "Chronic absenteeism rate"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_family = "sans", base_size = 8.8) +
  theme(
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "#fffefd", color = "#d8d2c5", linewidth = 0.45),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#fffefd", color = NA),
    plot.title = element_text(
      family = "sans",
      face = "bold",
      size = 10.2,
      color = ink,
      hjust = 0,
      margin = margin(b = 4)
    ),
    axis.title = element_text(
      family = "sans",
      face = "bold",
      size = 7.6,
      color = soft_ink
    ),
    axis.text = element_text(
      family = "sans",
      size = 7.2,
      color = soft_ink
    ),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "#e5dfd4", linewidth = 0.28),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.margin = margin(8, 10, 8, 10)
  )

relationship_plot <- ggplot(
  inset_data,
  aes(x = wildfire_student_days, y = pooled_chronic_absenteeism_rate)
) +
  annotate(
    "rect",
    xmin = county_fire_cutoff,
    xmax = Inf,
    ymin = county_absenteeism_median,
    ymax = Inf,
    fill = fire_orange,
    alpha = 0.08
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = county_fire_cutoff,
    color = "#8b8b8b",
    linewidth = 0.45,
    linetype = "dashed"
  ) +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = county_absenteeism_median,
    color = "#8b8b8b",
    linewidth = 0.45,
    linetype = "dashed"
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(fill = overlap_group),
    shape = 21,
    size = 4.4,
    color = "#ffffff",
    stroke = 0.55,
    alpha = 0.92
  ) +
  geom_segment(
    data = inset_data |> filter(!is.na(label)),
    aes(
      x = x_label,
      y = y_label,
      xend = wildfire_student_days,
      yend = pooled_chronic_absenteeism_rate
    ),
    linewidth = 0.28,
    color = "#777777"
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = inset_data |> filter(!is.na(label)),
    aes(x = x_label, y = y_label, label = label),
    family = "sans",
    fontface = "bold",
    size = 3.25,
    color = ink,
    lineheight = 0.95
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = county_fire_cutoff * 1.35,
    y = 0.455,
    hjust = 0,
    family = "sans",
    fontface = "bold",
    size = 3.8,
    color = fire_orange,
    label = "High wildfire burden\nand above-median absenteeism"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 0,
    y = county_absenteeism_median + 0.009,
    hjust = 0,
    family = "sans",
    size = 3.1,
    color = "#777777",
    label = "Median county absenteeism"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = county_fire_cutoff * 1.08,
    y = 0.052,
    angle = 90,
    hjust = 0,
    family = "sans",
    size = 3.1,
    color = "#777777",
    label = "Top wildfire-burden cutoff"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    trans = pseudo_log_trans(base = 10),
    breaks = c(0, 10000, 100000, 1000000),
    labels = label_short_count,
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.04, 0.18))
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0.04, 0.515),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.03))
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "High wildfire, high absenteeism" = "#8f3b25",
      "High wildfire, lower absenteeism" = "#d17a45",
      "Lower wildfire, high absenteeism" = "#326f77",
      "Lower wildfire, lower absenteeism" = "#b8b8b8"
    ),
    labels = c(
      "High wildfire, high absenteeism" = "High wildfire + high absenteeism",
      "High wildfire, lower absenteeism" = "High wildfire + lower absenteeism",
      "Lower wildfire, high absenteeism" = "Lower wildfire + high absenteeism",
      "Lower wildfire, lower absenteeism" = "Lower wildfire + lower absenteeism"
    ),
    name = NULL
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Wildfire missed student-days, 2017-18 and 2018-19",
    y = "Pooled chronic absenteeism rate"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_family = "sans", base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = paper, color = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = paper, color = NA),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "#e0d9ce", linewidth = 0.36),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_text(
      family = "sans",
      face = "bold",
      size = 11,
      color = soft_ink
    ),
    axis.text = element_text(
      family = "sans",
      size = 10.5,
      color = soft_ink
    ),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.justification = "left",
    legend.text = element_text(
      family = "sans",
      size = 10,
      color = soft_ink
    ),
    legend.key = element_rect(fill = paper, color = NA),
    plot.margin = margin(4, 22, 10, 18)
  )
```

```{r county-map-visualization, fig.width=13, fig.height=8, fig.alt="California county map showing chronic absenteeism by county color and wildfire missed student-days by proportional orange circles."}
grid.newpage()
grid.rect(gp = gpar(fill = paper, col = NA))
print(base_map, vp = viewport(x = 0.5, y = 0.455, width = 1, height = 0.76))
grid.text(
  map_title_text,
  x = 0.055,
  y = 0.965,
  just = c("left", "top"),
  gp = gpar(fontfamily = "sans", fontface = "bold", fontsize = 24, col = ink)
)
grid.text(
  map_subtitle_text,
  x = 0.055,
  y = 0.905,
  just = c("left", "top"),
  gp = gpar(fontfamily = "sans", fontsize = 13, col = "#3d3d3d", lineheight = 1.08)
)
grid.text(
  shared_caption_text,
  x = 0.055,
  y = 0.045,
  just = c("left", "bottom"),
  gp = gpar(fontfamily = "sans", fontsize = 8.5, col = "#6a6a6a", lineheight = 1.08)
)
```

```{r relationship-visualization, fig.width=13, fig.height=8, fig.alt="Scatterplot comparing county wildfire missed student-days with pooled chronic absenteeism rates, showing that high wildfire-burden counties are not always the highest chronic absenteeism counties."}
grid.newpage()
grid.rect(gp = gpar(fill = paper, col = NA))
print(relationship_plot, vp = viewport(x = 0.5, y = 0.455, width = 0.91, height = 0.70))
grid.text(
  relationship_title_text,
  x = 0.055,
  y = 0.965,
  just = c("left", "top"),
  gp = gpar(fontfamily = "sans", fontface = "bold", fontsize = 22, col = ink)
)
grid.text(
  relationship_subtitle_text,
  x = 0.055,
  y = 0.895,
  just = c("left", "top"),
  gp = gpar(fontfamily = "sans", fontsize = 13, col = "#3d3d3d", lineheight = 1.08)
)
grid.text(
  shared_caption_text,
  x = 0.055,
  y = 0.045,
  just = c("left", "bottom"),
  gp = gpar(fontfamily = "sans", fontsize = 8.5, col = "#6a6a6a", lineheight = 1.08)
)
```
